lede: A look at August 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tommy Mooney and Joseph Barbato, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2012 East Azerbaijan earthquakes
events.desc: At least 306 people are killed and 3,000 others injured in a pair of earthquakes near Tabriz, Iran.

events.title: Aeroflot
events.desc: Two Aeroflot Tupolev Tu-134s collide over the Ukrainian city of Dniprodzerzhynsk and crash, killing all 178 aboard both airliners.

events.title: Timor-Leste
events.desc: East Timor: Governor Mário Lemos Pires of Portuguese Timor abandons the capital Dili, following a coup by the Timorese Democratic Union (UDT) and the outbreak of civil war between UDT and Fretilin.

events.title: India
events.desc: The former Portuguese territories in India of Dadra and Nagar Haveli are merged to create the Union Territory Dadra and Nagar Haveli.

events.title: Kraków
events.desc: Poles in Kraków engage in a pogrom against Jews in the city, killing one and wounding five.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Amiens ends.

events.title: Colombia
events.desc: In Colombia, Juan del Corral declares the independence of Antioquia.

events.title: Francis Light
events.desc: Captain Francis Light establishes the British colony of Penang in Malaysia.

events.title: AD 106
events.desc: The south-western part of Dacia (modern Romania) becomes a Roman province: Roman Dacia.

births.name: Pervez Musharraf
births.line: Pakistani general and politician, 10th President of Pakistan

births.name: Peter Mohr Dam
births.line: Faroese educator and politician, 3rd Prime Minister of the Faroe Islands

deaths.text: V. S. Naipaul, British writer, Nobel Prize laureate
Alfred A. Knopf Sr., American publisher, founded Alfred A. Knopf, Inc.
Max Theiler, South African-American virologist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
John Henry Newman, English cardinal and theologian

observances.text: Christian Feast Day: John Henry Newman (Church of England)
